triageFlags.title: Applicant Age Below 21
triageFlags.description: The applicant's age is below the typical minimum age requirement for standard underwriting.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider requesting additional documentation to verify financial stability and maturity.
triageFlags.reasoning: The applicant's age of 19 is below the standard threshold of 21 years, which may indicate a higher risk due to limited financial experience.

triageFlags.title: High Loan-to-Value Ratio
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio exceeds the recommended maximum for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Evaluate the justification for the high LTV and consider requesting a higher deposit.
triageFlags.reasoning: The LTV ratio of 91.11% exceeds the policy's recommended maximum for Tier A applicants, which is typically 85%, indicating a higher risk exposure.

content.reasoning: The applicant's credit score and financials are strong, but the high LTV ratio and the applicant's young age introduce moderate risk. These factors warrant a cautious approach.
